prompt_text: Numeric vs FloQast: which is better?
response:
Numeric is generally better if you want **modern UI, deep analytics, and AI-driven automation at a lower cost**, while FloQast is typically better if you need a **battle‑tested close management platform with broad market adoption, strong support, and compliance focus**.

Below is a concise, side‑by‑side view to help you choose.

| Factor | **Numeric** | **FloQast** |
|---|---|---|
| Core strength | **Analytics + automation + AI** on top of close | **Close management + workflow + controls**[6] |
| Reporting & analytics | Highly flexible, **Excel‑level custom reports**; stronger flux customization and data analysis[1][4] | Good standard reports and flux, but more limited customization vs Numeric[1][4] |
| Reconciliations | **AI-powered auto‑matching (90%+ of transactions)**, end‑to‑end cash recs, 1‑click journal posting to NetSuite[1] | Strong reconciliation management; less emphasis (publicly) on auto‑matching % and GL‑to‑bank automation detail |
| Data & real‑time monitoring | **Continuous GL transaction sync**, real‑time alerts for errors/missing accruals/accounts out of balance[1] | Organizes close tasks and reconciliations; less marketed as a real‑time data monitoring tool[6] |
| AI capabilities | AI flux drafting, AI transaction matching, AI assistant for technical accounting memos[1] | **AI “agents”** positioned as teammates that run end‑to‑end workflows; FCA (FloQast Certified Accountant) program to train teams on AI[2] |
| UI & usability | Frequently praised as **more modern and intuitive** UI than FloQast[3][4] | Widely reviewed as **easy to use and set up**, but some users find UI less appealing vs Numeric[3][4] |
| Implementation | No setup fee; smaller, newer platform, often described as fast to implement and easy to adopt[1][3] | Entry‑level setup fee required; mature implementation playbook and broad customer base[2][3] |
| Market maturity & scale | Newer company with less total funding; strong momentum, especially among teams leaving FloQast[1] | **Founded 2013, Series E, $300M+ funding, $200M+ ARR, trusted by hundreds of public companies**[2] |
| Security & compliance | Positions as “accounting intelligence platform”; standard enterprise security (details usually in sales/security docs) | Strong emphasis on **ISO 42001**, governance, and AI oversight; pitched as highly secure and auditable[2] |
| Reviews & ratings | On TrustRadius and G2, Numeric scores are comparable or slightly higher on UX; users highlight better UI and value[3][4] | Very high satisfaction, G2 Leader for close/compliance/reconciliation; broader volume of reviews and references[2][4] |
| Pricing | Often cited as **more cost‑effective** with more included value at lower price[1][3] | Historically chosen for value at time of selection, but typically **higher TCO**, including setup fees[3] |

### When Numeric is likely *better* for you

Numeric is typically the better fit if:

- You want **flexible reporting and analytics**, not just checklist close:
  - Build **fully custom, Excel‑level reports**, group by any account/department/location/entity, and run detailed flux analysis in‑app.[1][4]  
- You care about **real‑time GL data quality and alerts**:
  - Continuous sync of every GL‑impacting transaction with alerts on erroneous entries, missing accruals, and out‑of‑balance accounts.[1]  
- You want **heavy automation of reconciliations and entries**:
  - AI rules engine matching **90%+ of transactions** on bank recs and drafting/posting hundreds of entries directly into NetSuite without CSVs.[1]  
- Your team values **modern UX** and ease of navigation:
  - Users explicitly say Numeric’s UI is more appealing and intuitive than FloQast, and “better overall” for their teams.[3][4]  
- You are cost‑sensitive:
  - No setup fee and multiple users note Numeric’s pricing is better and includes more for the price.[3]  

This tends to resonate with:
- Tech‑forward teams
- NetSuite‑centric environments
- Controllers/CFOs who want **analytics + close** in one place rather than just close tracking.

### When FloQast is likely *better* for you

FloQast is typically the better fit if:

- Your top priority is **structured, auditable close management**:
  - Very strong at organizing close tasks, reconciliations, ownership, and status tracking; many teams credit it with transforming their month‑end process
